communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all records naming or pertaining to Giovanni Edward McNab as identified below, and his dog Rigor, a male Anatolian Shepherd or ‘Kangal’, who were both shot and killed on August 9 of 2023, in a cabin located at 7299 E27 Mile Road, Goetzville, Michigan 49736-9417. Goetzville falls within Raber Township of Chippewa County in Michigans Upper Peninsula. 

Giovanni Edward McNab.
First five digits of SSN: 615-38;
DOB: February 22, 1989;
Date of Death: August 9, 2023.

Responsive records will include agents body cam footage, US C&BP reports, records of any prior border crossings, and/or investigative records (if any). Also records pertaining to the death of McNab's dog “Rigor”, an adult male Kangal (or Anatolian  herd dog) which was also shot and killed on August 9, 2023  at 7299 E27 Mile Road, Goetzville, Michigan 49736-9417.

Proof of Giovanni McNab’s death is attached. Also Law Enforcement press releases as well as public news reports were widely circulated announcing Giovanni McNabs death beginning on August 10th, so the privacy interests of deceased persons are widely acknowledged to be diminished; all responsive records should be released in the public interest. 

For cross-reference, the Michigan State Police incident report (Incident 080-0000132-23) indicated that the C&BP BORTAC agent who put Rigor down in order to mercifully end his suffering had a body worn camera.  

If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the CBP has no other copies of said records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Mike McNab
